title: Back up with the online backup API
description: Copy a live database with the backup API or VACUUM INTO, never a plain file copy, to avoid a corrupt snapshot.
body: Use when backing up a database that is being written. Do not cp the .db file while connections are open, especially in WAL mode where committed data lives partly in the -wal file. Actions: use the SQLite online backup API (sqlite3_backup_*), the .backup command, or VACUUM INTO 'backup.db' to produce a transactionally consistent single-file copy; if you must copy files, ensure all connections are closed/checkpointed first and include the -wal/-shm files. Validation signals include running PRAGMA integrity_check on the backup and restoring it successfully. Risk boundary: a naive live file copy can yield a backup that is silently corrupt or missing recent commits — verify every backup with integrity_check.
